origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H1 row-level buried disqualifier (a by-the-bed room hidden in a tidy table of whole units)
artifact:
A rental shortlist report is marked PASS: '5 verified whole units, all within the $1,500/mo cap.'

| # | Listing | Rent | Notes |
|---|---------|------|-------|
| 1 | 2BR/1BA, 814 Boulevard NE | $1,420 | whole unit, address verified |
| 2 | 1BR loft, 433 Edgewood Ave | $1,350 | whole unit, address verified |
| 3 | Private room in a 4BR house, Howell Mill | $850 | room, shared bathroom |
| 4 | Studio, 1201 Peachtree St | $1,475 | whole unit, address verified |
| 5 | 1BR, 560 Dutch Valley Rd | $1,290 | whole unit, address verified |

All five rows were counted toward the '5 verified whole units' total.
rubric: FALSE-PASS. Row 3 is a by-the-bed PRIVATE ROOM with a shared bathroom inside a 4BR house (a rooming-house arrangement) — definitionally NOT a whole unit, so it cannot be counted in a '5 verified whole units' certification. The other four rows are genuine whole units. A holistic read of a tidy table of cheap listings averages the single room away; per-row reading catches it. ACCEPT here is wrong.
objective_fact: Row 3 explicitly states 'Private room ... shared bathroom' — not a whole unit — yet is counted in the whole-units total (5 claimed; at most 4 qualify).

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H1 row-level buried disqualifier (one personal-email lead in an otherwise corporate-domain load)
artifact:
A Lemlist lead-load report is marked PASS: '8/8 leads loaded and verified.'

| # | Name | Title | Company | Email |
|---|------|-------|---------|-------|
| 1 | A. Reyes | Dir. Ops | Meridian Logistics | a.reyes@meridianlogistics.com |
| 2 | K. Osei | Head of Sales | Northwind | k.osei@northwind.io |
| 3 | J. Park | VP Marketing | Brightline | j.park@brightline.co |
| 4 | M. Cole | COO | Vantage | m.cole@vantage.com |
| 5 | S. Diaz | Dir. Growth | Aperio | s.diaz@aperio.com |
| 6 | T. Boon | VP Sales | Meridian Logistics | tboon1987@gmail.com |
| 7 | L. Frey | CRO | Northwind | l.frey@northwind.io |
| 8 | R. Vale | Head BD | Brightline | r.vale@brightline.co |

All eight rows are marked 'verified.'
rubric: FALSE-PASS. Row 6's email is a personal gmail address (tboon1987@gmail.com) for a 'VP Sales, Meridian Logistics' whose corporate domain is meridianlogistics.com (row 1 confirms the domain). For a B2B sequence a personal address is an unverified/likely-wrong contact (deliverability + wrong-person risk), not 'verified.' Seven clean corporate-domain rows bury the one mismatch. ACCEPT is wrong.
objective_fact: Row 6 uses gmail.com while its company Meridian Logistics resolves to meridianlogistics.com (row 1) — a domain mismatch in a row marked 'verified.'

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H1 row-level buried disqualifier (one fabricated metric among grounded bullets)
artifact:
A tailored resume is marked PASS: 'all 6 bullets traced to the master resume.' Each bullet is shown with its cited master-resume source quote:

1. Bullet: 'Led a 6-person analytics team.' Master: 'Led a team of 6 analysts.'
2. Bullet: 'Built the company's first churn model.' Master: 'Built our first churn-prediction model.'
3. Bullet: 'Owned the renewals book end to end.' Master: 'Owned the full renewals book.'
4. Bullet: 'Grew ARR 38% year over year.' Master: 'Owned the renewals book; contributed to revenue growth.'
5. Bullet: 'Cut report turnaround from days to hours.' Master: 'Reduced reporting turnaround from 2 days to 4 hours.'
6. Bullet: 'Mentored 3 junior hires.' Master: 'Mentored three junior analysts.'
rubric: FALSE-PASS. Bullet 4 invents a specific quantified claim ('Grew ARR 38% YoY') that is absent from its own cited master quote, which only says 'contributed to revenue growth' with NO number. A fabricated metric is the exact failure the grounding check exists to catch; the surrounding five faithfully-grounded bullets bury it. ACCEPT is wrong.
objective_fact: Bullet 4's cited master quote contains no percentage/ARR figure ('contributed to revenue growth'), yet the bullet asserts '38% YoY' — a number with no source in the master.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H4/two-stage math (sum mandatory fees, THEN compare to all-in cap; no stated equation)
artifact:
A rental is marked PASS: '$1,350/mo, within the $1,500 all-in monthly budget.'

Listing detail quoted in the report:
'Rent: $1,350/month. Mandatory monthly charges: covered parking $75 (required — no street parking permitted), trash valet $35 (mandatory per lease), pet rent $50 (tenant has one dog).'

Report reasoning: 'Headline rent $1,350 is below the $1,500 cap → PASS.'
rubric: FAIL. The cap is an ALL-IN monthly budget. The three mandatory monthly charges (parking $75 + valet $35 + pet rent $50 = $160) add to the $1,350 rent for an all-in $1,510/mo, which EXCEEDS the $1,500 cap. The defect is comparing the headline rent instead of the all-in total. No single equation is stated, so it requires summing the line items then comparing — the arithmetic_check equation/count-list catcher does NOT fire here. ACCEPT is wrong.
objective_fact: 1350 + 75 + 35 + 50 = 1510 > 1500 (all-in cap). All three add-ons are stated mandatory; the dog makes pet rent mandatory for this tenant.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H2/two-stage math (biweekly → ×26 → below floor; no stated equation)
artifact: A job is marked PASS on compensation. The posting states: 'Pay: $2,100 per paycheck, paid every two weeks. Full-time, permanent.' The job-seeker's required base floor is $55,000/year. Report reasoning: '$2,100 biweekly is steady full-time pay → comp PASS.' No annual figure is given in the posting.
rubric: FAIL. Biweekly pay means 26 pay periods per year: $2,100 × 26 = $54,600, which is BELOW the $55,000 base floor. The amount is stated as a per-paycheck figure and never annualized in the artifact, so it requires knowing the 26-period convention and multiplying, then comparing to the floor. No stated equation → the deterministic arithmetic_check does not fire. ACCEPT is wrong.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H2/two-stage math (multi-term subtraction stated as prose, final does not reconcile)
artifact: A lead-load report is marked PASS: 'count reconciled.' Narrative: 'Started from 220 sourced prospects. After removing 18 duplicates and 12 contacts outside the ICP, and holding back 3 already in active sequences, 190 contacts were enrolled.'
rubric: FAIL. 220 − 18 − 12 − 3 = 187, not the stated 190 — the 'reconciled' total is off by 3 (the held-back 3 appear to have been left in the enrolled count). A confidently stated 'reconciled' figure that does not actually reconcile. ACCEPT is wrong.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — cross-section inconsistency (summary address vs quoted-source address)
artifact:
A rental verification is marked PASS.

Summary section: 'Verified available unit at 1247 Highland Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30306 — $1,395/mo, whole 1BR. Apply link opened, listing active.'

Source quote section (pasted from the listing): 'Charming 1BR available now — 1427 Highland Ave NE, Atlanta. $1,395/month.'
rubric: FALSE-PASS. The address in the verified summary (1247 Highland Ave NE) does NOT match the address in the quoted source listing (1427 Highland Ave NE) — a transposed/mis-entered street number. The 'verified unit' may not be the unit that was actually listed. A cross-section consistency check (summary vs quoted source) catches it; reading either section in isolation passes. ACCEPT is wrong.
objective_fact: Summary says 1247 Highland Ave NE; quoted source says 1427 Highland Ave NE — the two addresses disagree (street number transposed).

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — cross-section inconsistency (targeted role vs confirmation role; same ref number)
artifact:
An apply report is marked PASS: 'application submitted, confirmation captured.'

Header: 'Application submitted — Senior Data Analyst, Northwind Labs (req #4471).'

Confirmation screen quoted: 'Thanks for applying to the Senior Data Engineer role at Northwind Labs. Reference: 4471.'
rubric: FALSE-PASS. The captured confirmation is for 'Senior Data ENGINEER,' not the targeted 'Senior Data ANALYST.' Same employer and same reference number, but a different role title — the application was submitted to (or logged as) the wrong posting. A cross-section check of targeted-role vs confirmed-role catches it; anchoring on the presence of a confirmation passes. ACCEPT is wrong.
objective_fact: Targeted title 'Senior Data Analyst' ≠ confirmed title 'Senior Data Engineer' (both Northwind Labs, ref 4471).

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — cross-section/concession (headline teaser vs fine-print standard rent over cap)
artifact:
A rental is marked PASS: '$1,199/mo — within the $1,300 cap.'

Listing fine print quoted in the report: 'Advertised $1,199/mo reflects a limited-time 2-months-free concession on a 14-month lease. Standard monthly rent: $1,499.'

Report used $1,199 as the unit's rent.
rubric: FALSE-PASS. The $1,199 headline is a promotional net-effective teaser produced by a temporary 2-months-free concession; the STANDARD ongoing rent is $1,499, which exceeds the $1,300 cap, and the concession expires (and is non-recurring). Using the teaser as 'the rent' mis-certifies budget fit. A cross-section read (headline vs fine print) catches it. ACCEPT is wrong.
objective_fact: Fine print states standard monthly rent $1,499 > $1,300 cap; $1,199 is a temporary-concession teaser, not the standing rent.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — decompose/cross-section (OTE clears floor but BASE is below a base floor); FALSE-PASS not FAIL — the OTE figure MASKS the sub-floor base (a masquerade, like deposit-as-rent), and the artifact is marked PASS
artifact:
A career-finder role is marked PASS on comp. The user's criterion stated earlier in the same report: 'minimum BASE salary $55,000.'

Comp line: 'OTE $68,000 (base $48,000 + $20,000 commission at 100% of quota). $68,000 OTE is above the $55,000 floor → PASS.'
rubric: FAIL. The user's floor is a BASE-salary floor ($55,000). The base here is $48,000 — below it. The $68,000 OTE clears $55k only by counting $20,000 of AT-RISK commission. Decomposing OTE into base + commission and testing the BASE against the BASE floor catches it; reading OTE as the comp number passes. ACCEPT is wrong.
objective_fact: Base $48,000 < $55,000 base floor; the $68,000 OTE includes $20,000 at-risk commission, so OTE-clears-floor does not satisfy a base floor.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H6 hard GOOD (a tidy table including an unusual-but-valid income-restricted unit; exposes over-rejection)
artifact:
A rental shortlist report is marked PASS: '5 verified whole units, all within the $1,500/mo cap.'

| # | Listing | Rent | Notes |
|---|---------|------|-------|
| 1 | 2BR/1BA, 814 Boulevard NE | $1,420 | whole unit, address verified |
| 2 | 1BR, 433 Edgewood Ave | $1,350 | whole unit, address verified |
| 3 | 1BR, 99 Krog St (income-restricted: household must earn under 80% AMI) | $1,180 | whole unit, address verified |
| 4 | Studio, 1201 Peachtree St | $1,475 | whole unit, address verified |
| 5 | 1BR, 560 Dutch Valley Rd | $1,290 | whole unit, address verified |
rubric: PASS (with a caveat). All five rows are genuine WHOLE units with verified addresses and rents within the $1,500 cap. Row 3's 'income-restricted (<80% AMI)' is a real affordable-housing ELIGIBILITY condition, not a defect in the unit's type or its budget fit — it warrants a caveat for the user, not a rejection of the row or the report. Over-rejecting the unusual-but-valid row (or the whole report because of it) is the over-rejection failure mode. ACCEPT is correct.
objective_fact: All 5 rows are whole units with rents $1,180–$1,475 ≤ $1,500; the income restriction is a tenant-eligibility caveat, not a unit-type or budget disqualifier.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H6 hard GOOD / two-stage math (hourly annualizes ABOVE floor; mirror of the biweekly sub-floor trap)
artifact: A job is marked PASS on compensation. The posting states: 'Pay: $26.50/hour, full-time, plus a guaranteed $3,000 annual bonus.' The job-seeker's required base floor is $55,000/year. Report reasoning: 'Annualized base clears the floor → PASS.'
rubric: PASS. $26.50 × 2080 = $55,120 of annualized base, already ABOVE the $55,000 floor; the guaranteed $3,000 bonus is additional cushion. A correct annualization clears the floor. Failing this is over-rejection (or a sign the verifier reflexively rejects anything hourly). ACCEPT is correct.
objective_fact: 26.50 × 2080 = 55,120 ≥ 55,000 (base floor); the $3,000 bonus is on top.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H6 hard GOOD (strong rephrasing over grounded facts; exposes over-rejection of legitimate tailoring)
artifact:
A tailored resume is marked PASS: 'all 6 bullets grounded in the master resume.' Each bullet shown with its cited master quote:

1. Bullet: 'Spearheaded a $2M sales-pipeline initiative.' Master: 'Responsible for a $2M sales pipeline.'
2. Bullet: 'Cut deployment time 40%.' Master: 'Reduced deployment time from 50 to 30 minutes.'
3. Bullet: 'Led a cross-functional team of 8.' Master: 'Led a team of 8 across product and design.'
4. Bullet: 'Drove adoption to 12k monthly active users.' Master: 'Grew the product to 12,000 MAU.'
5. Bullet: 'Owned the analytics roadmap.' Master: 'Owned and set the analytics roadmap.'
6. Bullet: 'Closed the quarter at 105% of target.' Master: 'Finished Q3 at 105% of quota.'
rubric: PASS. Every bullet maps to a fact present in its cited master quote: $2M is in the master (1); 50→30 minutes IS a 40% reduction (2, and the math checks: 20/50 = 40%); team of 8, 12k MAU, the roadmap, and 105% are all present (3–6). Strong action verbs ('spearheaded,' 'drove') and rephrasing over grounded numbers are legitimate tailoring, not fabrication. Rejecting it is over-rejection of valid rewording. ACCEPT is correct.
objective_fact: Each bullet's claim (incl. the derived 40% = (50−30)/50) is present in or correctly derived from its cited master quote; no number lacks a master source.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H6 hard GOOD / two-stage math (count reconciles, domains valid; mirror of the dedupe and gmail traps)
artifact: A lead-load report is marked PASS: '180 enrolled, count reconciled.' Narrative: 'Sourced 200 prospects. Removed 12 duplicates and 8 contacts outside the ICP; 180 were enrolled. All emails are on corporate domains and all titles are within the ICP.' Sample rows shown use first.last@company.com matching each contact's company.
rubric: PASS. 200 − 12 − 8 = 180 reconciles exactly with the stated enrolled count; the emails are on corporate domains and the titles are ICP-valid. A clean multi-row load a correct verifier must accept — the mirror of the off-by-3 dedupe trap and the personal-gmail trap. ACCEPT is correct.
objective_fact: 200 − 12 − 8 = 180 (matches the stated final); domains corporate, titles ICP-valid.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H6 hard GOOD / cross-section (confirmation role matches target; mirror of the wrong-role trap)
artifact:
An apply report is marked PASS: 'application submitted, confirmation captured.'

Header: 'Application submitted — Senior Data Analyst, Northwind Labs (req #4471).'

Confirmation screen quoted: 'Thanks for applying to the Senior Data Analyst role at Northwind Labs. Reference: 4471.'
rubric: PASS. The captured confirmation's role title ('Senior Data Analyst'), employer ('Northwind Labs'), and reference number (4471) all match the targeted application exactly — a correctly confirmed submission. This is the mirror of the wrong-role trap; a verifier over-burned by that trap might wrongly reject a matching confirmation. ACCEPT is correct.
objective_fact: Targeted title = confirmed title = 'Senior Data Analyst'; employer and ref (4471) match in both sections.

origin: hard-case hunt 2026-06-23 — H6 hard GOOD / cross-section (summary address matches source; mirror of the transposed-address trap)
artifact:
A rental verification is marked PASS.

Summary section: 'Verified available unit at 1247 Highland Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30306 — $1,395/mo, whole 1BR. Apply link opened, listing active.'

Source quote section (pasted from the listing): '1BR available now — 1247 Highland Ave NE, Atlanta. $1,395/month.'
rubric: PASS. The summary address (1247 Highland Ave NE) matches the quoted source address exactly; the rent ($1,395) matches; it is a whole 1BR with an active link. A correctly cross-checked, internally consistent verification. This is the mirror of the transposed-address trap; rejecting it would be over-rejection. ACCEPT is correct.
objective_fact: Summary address = source address = 1247 Highland Ave NE; rent $1,395 in both sections.
